perturbation.original_text: 11. The Recipient will, within five business days of a written request by the Disclosing Party, confirm that it has destroyed the original provided to Recipient (and all copies made by it) any documents (in whatever Project Bell – Non-disclosure Agreement Page 3 of 6 form or medium) provided, or made available for review by, the Disclosing Party (including any Derivative Confidential Information). Notwithstanding the immediately preceding sentence, the parties agree that destruction will not be made of (i) any electronic copy of any Confidential Information that was created pursuant to the standard electronic backup and archival procedures of the Recipient if (x) personnel of the Recipient whose functions are not primarily internal information technology in nature do not have access to such retained copies and (y) personnel of the Recipient whose functions are primarily internal information technology in nature have access to such copies only as reasonably necessary for the performance of their internal information technology duties ( e.g. , for purposes of system recovery). The parties further agree that they will continue to treat as confidential in accordance with the Agreement, any Confidential Information retained pursuant to the second sentence of this Section 11.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. The Recipient will, within five business days of a written request by the Disclosing Party, confirm that it has destroyed the original provided to Recipient any documents provided, or made available for review by, the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the parenthetical phrase '(and all copies made by it) (in whatever Project Bell – Non-disclosure Agreement Page 3 of 6 form or medium) (including any Derivative Confidential Information)' and the subsequent clause regarding electronic copies and archival procedures introduces ambiguity regarding what exactly needs to be destroyed. The in-text contradiction occurs because the remainder of Section 11 discusses the continued confidentiality of retained information, assuming that such a retention policy is in effect. By removing that policy, the text is now contradictory. The clause that says they will continue to treat as confidential in accordance with the Agreement doesn't apply because there is no electronic copy to treat as confidential
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. The Recipient will, within five business days of a written request by the Disclosing Party, confirm that it has destroyed the original provided to Recipient any documents provided, or made available for review by, the Disclosing Party. The parties further agree that they will continue to treat as confidential in accordance with the Agreement, any Confidential Information retained pursuant to the second sentence of this Section 11.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the explicit allowance for retaining electronic copies of confidential information, which directly contradicts the contradicted text that states, 'The parties further agree that they will continue to treat as confidential in accordance with the Agreement, any Confidential Information retained pursuant to the second sentence of this Section 11.' Since the second sentence regarding electronic copies has been removed, the agreement to treat those copies as confidential is now logically inconsistent because there is no retained information that the agreement now applies to, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 13. The Recipient agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for an actual or threatened breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or its Representatives, and that any court having competent jurisdiction may enter a preliminary and/or permanent restraining order, injunction or order for specific performance, without posting any bond, in the event of an actual or threatened breach of any of the provisions of this Agreement, in addition to any other remedy at law or in equity available to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. The Recipient agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for an actual or threatened breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or its Representatives, and that any court having competent jurisdiction may enter a preliminary restraining order in the event of an actual or threatened breach of any of the provisions of this Agreement, in addition to any other remedy at law or in equity available to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the mention of 'permanent restraining order, injunction or order for specific performance, without posting any bond,' the clause is severely weakened. The Disclosing Party loses key avenues for seeking immediate and decisive action. In-text contradiction occurs because the agreement specifies in multiple places, that irreparable harm may occur, but removes the ability to have a permanent injunction for damages to cease.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. The Recipient will keep, and will cause its Representatives to keep, the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party confidential. The Recipient will not disclose the Confidential Information or use or permit the Confidential Information to be disclosed or used, to or by any person except Recipient’s Representatives, in each case, who agree to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. In any such case Confidential Information will be disclosed only to those Representatives with a need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of the Proposed Transaction, and (iv) only so much of the Confidential Information as is necessary for a particular individual to perform his or her function will be disclosed to that individual. Under no circumstances shall the Recipient or its Representatives use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in any manner or for any purpose other than evaluating and negotiating the Proposed Transaction. In any event, the Recipient will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for 'a preliminary and/or permanent restraining order, injunction or order for specific performance, without posting any bond'. The changed text removes 'permanent restraining order, injunction or order for specific performance, without posting any bond' only allowing a 'preliminary restraining order'. This contradicts the contradicted text, which implies the need to keep confidential information safe, and could imply the need for a permanent solution if that information is leaked or in danger of being leaked. The omission of the ability to get a permanent injunction creates a situation where the 'irreparable harm' from losing confidential information cannot be stopped, as only a preliminary injunction is available. This meets the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 20. To the extent that any Confidential Information includes materials subject to the attorney-client privilege or similar privileges or protections, such disclosure is inadvertent and the Disclosing Party is not waiving, and shall not be deemed to have waived or diminished, its attorney work product protections, attorney-client privileges or similar protections and privileges as a result of disclosing any Confidential Information (including Confidential Information related to pending or threatened litigation) to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 20. To the extent that any Confidential Information includes materials subject to the attorney-client privilege or similar privileges or protections, such disclosure is inadvertent and the Disclosing Party is not waiving, and shall not be deemed to have waived or diminished, its attorney work product protections, attorney-client privileges or similar protections and privileges.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'as a result of disclosing any Confidential Information (including Confidential Information related to pending or threatened litigation) to the Receiving Party' the text becomes contradictory. It removes the direct relation of the attorny-client privilege being kept to disclosing any confidential information to the Receiving Party. This text creates a contradiction because now it is not clear if disclosing any information waives the privilege.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. If the Recipient, or anyone else to whom Confidential Information is provided as permitted by this Agreement, receives notice indicating that it may or will be legally compelled to disclose any of the Confidential Information, Recipient will make a reasonable effort to provide the Disclosing Party with prompt notice so that the Disclosing Party may seek, at its expense, a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with this Agreement. If a protective order or other remedy is not obtained for whatever reason, or if the Disclosing Party waives compliance with this Agreement, the compelled person will furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information which Recipient is informed by advice of its counsel that it is required to disclose.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that disclosing confidential information to the Receiving Party did not waive attorney-client privilege. The changed text removes the phrase 'as a result of disclosing any Confidential Information (including Confidential Information related to pending or threatened litigation) to the Receiving Party,' thus removing the guarantee that disclosing information to the Receiving Party preserves the privilege. The contradicted text (section 8) outlines a process for the Recipient to follow if compelled to disclose confidential information, including the possibility of waiving compliance with the agreement. Because the modified text omits the specific assurance that disclosure to the receiving party does not waive attorney-client privilege, this creates a contradiction. The omitted language creates ambiguity about whether the Disclosing Party waives compliance to the agreement which contrasts the clear process in Section 8, thus it creates a strong contradiction.
